message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "infra-fit" reviewer, severity critical:
TITLE: gha-outrunner is an unverified load-bearing dependency; the Rust skeleton itself does almost nothing
DETAIL: The entire proposal wraps 'gha-outrunner', but the skeleton's runner.rs is placeholders ('Starting gha-outrunner (placeholder)...'), service install is 'not yet implemented', and backend.rs assumes 'Sysbox is installed or will be'. All the hard parts — runner registration, ephemeral lifecycle, VM image management, scale sets — are delegated to a tool whose existence, maturity, and maintenance status the proposal never establishes. The actual novel code in the skeleton is ~30 lines of platform detection (/dev/kvm exists, `which tart`) and a YAML heredoc — trivially expressible in bash. Compare: the existing stack's value is not its topology but ~2 years of encoded failure knowledge across self-hosted-oss/heal-runners.sh, preflight.sh, launchd-start.sh, linux/install.sh, and self-hosted-colima/scripts/monitor.sh.
RECOMMENDATION: Before any further design work, verify gha-outrunner exists, supports org-scope runners + GitHub Scale Sets, and is maintained. If it does not, the proposal collapses to 'write a runner orchestrator from scratch in Rust', which is a very different (and much larger) decision. Evaluate established alternatives first: actions-runner-controller (k8s), cirruslabs Tart-based runner tooling on macOS, or GitHub's official ephemeral-runner APIs driven from the existing scripts.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about "outrunner":

outrunner is a tool that provides ephemeral VM and container runners for GitHub Actions without requiring Kubernetes, offering the same isolation guarantees with Docker, libvirt, or Tart.

The search results show that outrunner is a project by NetwindHQ that simplifies GitHub Actions runner management. It's designed for users running on bare metal or a simple VPS who shouldn't need a cluster just to get ephemeral runners.

The search results also reveal related tools and approaches:
- Tart is a project built on top of Apple's Virtualization framework by providing both a GUI and CLI for managing VMs
- libvirt can be used to run GitHub Actions workflows in a virtual machine

All three technologies (outrunner, Tart, and libvirt) are used in the GitHub Actions self-hosted runner ecosystem to provide flexible, isolated environments for running workflows.

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner: Ephemeral VM and container runners for GitHub Actions — no Kubernetes required","url":"https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner"},{"title":"Get started with Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/tutorials/use-actions-runner-controller/get-started"},{"title":"GitHub - rust-lang/gha-runner: Custom fork of the GitHub Actions runner","url":"https://github.com/rust-lang/gha-runner"},{"title":"GitHub - chaddyc/gha-runner: GitHub Actions Runner Docker Container · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/chaddyc/gha-runner"},{"title":"Deploying runner scale sets with Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/manage-runners/use-actions-runner-controller/deploy-runner-scale-sets"},{"title":"Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/runners/actions-runner-controller"},{"title":"GitHub - omsf/gha-runner: A simple GitHub Action for creating cloud-based self-hosted runners. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/omsf/gha-runner"},{"title":"GitHub - PasseiDireto/gh-runner: Self Hosted GitHub Actions Runner · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/PasseiDireto/gh-runner"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about **gha-outrunner**:

## Overview

gha-outrunner provides ephemeral GitHub Actions runners without requiring Kubernetes, provisioning fresh Docker containers or VMs for each GitHub Actions job and destroying them when the job completes.

## Key Features

- **No Kubernetes Required**: If you're running on bare metal or a simple VPS, outrunner gives you the same isolation guarantees with Docker, libvirt, or Tart without needing an additional orchestrator.

- **Multiple Backend Options**: The tool supports container per job with fastest startup, KVM VMs from qcow2 golden images with CoW overlays using QEMU Guest Agent for command execution, and Tart VM clones per job with Tart guest agent for command execution.

- **GitHub's Scaleset API**: It uses GitHub's scaleset API to register as an autoscaling runner group.

The project is developed by **NetwindHQ** and is available on GitHub at https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner. It offers a lightweight alternative to GitHub's Actions Runner Controller (ARC) for organizations that don't want to deploy a Kubernetes cluster just to run ephemeral runners.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: The tool exists. Let me check its maturity and maintenance status.

input.prompt: Describe this project: stars, forks, last commit date, release count/latest release, language, whether it supports org-level runners, GitHub scale sets API, ephemeral runners, backends (docker, libvirt, tart), number of contributors, open issues. Any signs of maturity or immaturity.

content.content:
# gha-outrunner Project Analysis

**Repository Metrics:**
- Stars: 3
- Forks: 0
- Latest Release: v1.2.0 (April 20, 2026)
- Release Count: 5 total releases
- Primary Language: Go (95.9%)

**Core Functionality:**
This project provides "ephemeral GitHub Actions runners, no Kubernetes required." It provisions fresh Docker containers or VMs for each job, then destroys them upon completion using GitHub's scaleset API.

**Supported Backends:**
- Docker (Linux, macOS host → Linux runner)
- libvirt/KVM (Linux host → Windows/Linux VMs)
- Tart (macOS Apple Silicon → macOS/Linux ARM64 VMs)

**Key Features:**
- GitHub scale sets API integration for autoscaling runner groups
- No orchestration platform required
- Multiple provisioning options for different infrastructure needs

**Maturity Indicators:**

*Positive Signs:*
- 5 releases with semantic versioning
- Documented architecture, security model, and provisioner details
- Setup guides for multiple platforms
- Contributing and security policy documentation

*Concerns:*
- Only 3 stars and 0 forks suggest limited adoption
- No visible contributor information in provided data
- 17 open issues
- Single author attribution

**Support Status:**
Built by Paweł Subocz at Netwind; at least one known production user (delo.so).

input.reasoning: The finding's core factual claims all verify against the doc and repo. (1) The skeleton quotes are verbatim accurate: runner.rs prints "Starting gha-outrunner (placeholder)..." with the real invocation commented out, InstallService prints "Service installation not yet implemented", and backend.rs contains the comment "assume Sysbox is installed or will be". (2) The doc genuinely never establishes gha-outrunner's existence, repo, author, maturity, or maintenance status — it is treated as a given. (3) The novel code really is ~30 lines of platform detection (/dev/kvm check, which::which("tart")) plus a YAML heredoc. (4) All five cited existing-stack files exist in the repo (1,475 lines total, 88 commits). Adversarial web verification of the recommendation's key question: gha-outrunner DOES exist (github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner, Go, v1.2.0 released 2026-04-20, 5 releases, Docker/libvirt/Tart backends, GitHub scale sets API) — so the worst-case "proposal collapses to writing an orchestrator from scratch" branch does not fire. However, the tool has 3 stars, 0 forks, a single author, and 17 open issues — near-zero adoption — which substantiates rather than refutes the "unverified load-bearing dependency" concern for critical CI infrastructure. Severity 'critical' is defensible for an infra-fit review: the design bets the entire runner fleet on an unevaluated single-maintainer project while the wrapper itself adds almost nothing. Two supporting details are wrong: the existing stack's git history in this repo starts 2026-03-02 (~4 months, not "~2 years" of encoded failure knowledge), and the recommendation's existence question is now answered affirmatively. These errors do not overturn the finding's substance, so it is not refuted; a corrected version is provided.
input.corrected_claim: gha-outrunner IS a real, recently maintained project (github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner — Go, v1.2.0 April 2026, Docker/libvirt/Tart backends, GitHub scale sets API), so the proposal does not collapse outright — but it has essentially zero adoption (3 stars, 0 forks, single author, 17 open issues), so treating it as the load-bearing dependency for all CI runners without any evaluation remains a critical infra-fit risk. The rest of the finding stands as written: the Rust skeleton is placeholders (runner start, service install) plus ~30 lines of platform detection and a YAML heredoc, and all hard parts (registration, ephemeral lifecycle, VM images, scale sets) are delegated to the unevaluated tool. One correction: the existing stack (self-hosted-oss/*.sh + self-hosted-colima/scripts/monitor.sh, 1,475 lines, 88 commits) encodes roughly 4 months of operational failure knowledge (first commit 2026-03-02), not ~2 years. The recommendation should be updated from "verify it exists" to "evaluate its production-readiness and bus-factor against alternatives (actions-runner-controller, cirruslabs Tart tooling, GitHub's ephemeral-runner APIs driven from the existing scripts) before adopting it."
